login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Authenticate by extracting JWT from the running Descript app. The Descript desktop app must be open. Use --force/-F to clear the token cache and re-authenticate.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth logout --help` for exact arguments and options.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Check if a valid JWT token is available. Shows whether token is cached or needs refresh from the running app.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth test --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles list --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles get --help` for exact arguments and options.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles create --help` for exact arguments and options.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles set-default --help` for exact arguments and options.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles delete --help` for exact arguments and options.

profiles.usage_instructions: Run `descript auth profiles --help` for exact arguments and options.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management. Extracts JWT token from the running Descript desktop app. The Descript app must be open for login to work.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `descript cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `descript cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

list.help: List compositions in a project. Example: descript compositions list <project-id> descript compositions list <project-id> --table descript compositions list <project-id> --filter "name:m1c1"
list.usage_instructions: List all compositions in a project. PROJECT_ID is a required positional argument (UUID). Use --filter/-f to search by name (e.g., 'name:contains:m1c1'). Default limit is 100.

get.help: Get a specific composition from a project. Example: descript compositions get <project-id> <composition-id>
get.usage_instructions: Get details for a specific composition. Requires both PROJECT_ID and COMPOSITION_ID as positional arguments (UUIDs).

export.help: Export a video asset or composition as MP4. Two export modes: 1. Asset export (raw recording via API): descript compositions export <project-id> <asset-id> 2. Composition export (full video with slides via Descript app): descript compositions export <project-id> --composition m2c1 -o ./m2c1.mp4 Composition export requires Descript to be running with the project open. It automates the app's local export to produce the full rendered video. Example: descript compositions export <project-id> <asset-id> descript compositions export <project-id> --composition m2c1 -o ./m2c1.mp4 descript compositions export <project-id> <asset-id> --fps 60 --width 3840 --height 2160

arguments.name: asset_id
arguments.help: Video asset ID for raw export (use 'descript compositions assets' to find)

options.name: --composition
options.help: Composition name or ID to export via Descript app

export.usage_instructions: Export video in two modes: (1) Asset export (raw recording via API) — pass PROJECT_ID and ASSET_ID as positional args. (2) Composition export (full rendered video with slides) — pass PROJECT_ID and use --composition/-C with the composition name or ID; requires Descript app to be running with the project open. Full composition exports render through the Descript desktop app, and the CLI waits up to 30 minutes after accepting the save dialog before timing out. Default resolution is 1920x1080 at 30fps.

assets.help: List video assets in a project for export. Shows the raw video recordings available in the project. Use the asset ID with 'descript compositions export' to download. Example: descript compositions assets <project-id> descript compositions assets <project-id> --table
assets.usage_instructions: List raw video recording assets in a project. Use the asset ID from this output with `compositions export` to download raw recordings. PROJECT_ID is a required positional argument.

compositions.usage_instructions: Manage compositions within Descript projects. List compositions, get details, list video assets, and export videos. PROJECT_ID (UUID) is required for all operations.

start.help: Start monitoring Descript network activity. Captures all API calls made by the Descript app for endpoint discovery and debugging. Writes to ~/.descript/monitor.log. Example: descript monitor start descript monitor start --foreground

options.name: --foreground
options.help: Run in foreground instead of background

start.usage_instructions: Start capturing Descript app API calls in the background. Use --foreground/-F to run in foreground instead. Logs to ~/.descript/monitor.log.

stop.help: Stop the network monitor. Example: descript monitor stop
stop.usage_instructions: Stop the background network monitor process.

status.help: Check if the monitor is running. Example: descript monitor status
status.usage_instructions: Check whether the network monitor is currently running.

tail.help: Show recent monitor log output. Example: descript monitor tail descript monitor tail -n 100 descript monitor tail --follow
tail.usage_instructions: Show recent lines from the monitor log. Default is 50 lines. Use --lines/-n to change count. Use --follow/-f for live streaming.

monitor.usage_instructions: Monitor and debug Descript app network activity. Captures all API calls for endpoint discovery and debugging. Logs to ~/.descript/monitor.log.

list.help: List your Descript projects. Example: descript projects list descript projects list --limit 10 descript projects list --table descript projects list --filter "name:contains:Cursor" descript projects list -p "name,composition_count" descript projects list -p "name,owner.email"

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of projects to list

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated fields to display (supports dot notation: owner.email)

list.usage_instructions: List all Descript projects. Default limit is 50. Use --filter/-f to search by name (e.g., 'name:contains:Cursor'). Use --properties/-p with dot notation for nested fields (e.g., 'name,owner.email').

get.help: Get a specific project by ID. Example: descript projects get <project-id> descript projects get <project-id> --table
get.usage_instructions: Get full details for a project. PROJECT_ID is a required positional argument (UUID).

projects.usage_instructions: List and inspect Descript projects. Use `projects list` to find project IDs needed for composition and export operations.

usage_instructions: Use for managing Descript projects, compositions, and video exports. Commands follow `descript <group> <action>` pattern. Auth requires the Descript desktop app to be running. Most list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p.
